merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations established with Kazakhstan on December 25, 1991, following U.S. recognition of Kazakhstan's independence upon dissolution of the Soviet Union. No mission yet established.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were established on December 25, 1991, when President George H.W. Bush announced the decision in an address to the nation regarding the dissolution of the Soviet Union.

merged_events.event_description: The American Embassy in Alma-Ata (now Almaty) was established on February 3, 1992, with William Harrison Courtney as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: The American Embassy in Alma-Ata (now Almaty) was established on February 3, 1992, with William Harrison Courtney as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Embassy established in Kazakhstan when the first Ambassador, William Harrison Courtney, presented credentials.

evidence.quote: William Harrison Courtney, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1992-08-11. Credentials presented: 1992-09-15. Left post on 1995-07-01.

matched.notes: CSV date 1992-02-03 matches RDCR (Event 1) exactly for Embassy establishment. POCOM (Event 2) gives 1992-09-15, which is the credential presentation date for the first Ambassador — a later personnel event. Per source priority, RDCR's institutional date is correct and the CSV follows it.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Diplomatic relations established with Kazakhstan on December 25, 1991, when President George H.W. Bush announced U.S. recognition of Kazakhstan's independence upon dissolution of the Soviet Union. No mission yet established.
missing_from_csv.notes: High-confidence RDCR event. This represents the initial establishment of diplomatic relations prior to the opening of the Embassy. Candidate for addition as a 'None' status event preceding the Embassy establishment.
